phases.name: subquery_scan_aggregate
phases.description: Sequential scan lineitem (sorted by l_orderkey) to compute SUM(l_quantity) per orderkey, keep orderkeys with sum > 300

phases.name: index_lookup_orders
phases.description: For each qualifying orderkey, lookup orders row via dense index to get o_custkey, o_orderdate, o_totalprice

phases.name: index_lookup_customer
phases.description: For each qualifying order, lookup customer row via dense index to get c_custkey (c_name deferred to final output)

phases.name: sort_and_limit
phases.description: Sort ~15K results by o_totalprice DESC, o_orderdate ASC; take top 100; decode c_name only for top 100

join_order.notes: Subquery produces ~15K qualifying orderkeys; orders_orderkey_lookup gives O(1) access
customer_custkey_lookup gives O(1) row access by custkey

data_structures.subquery_aggregation: sorted_sequential_accumulate (no hash table needed — l_orderkey sorted)
data_structures.qualifying_results: flat_array<struct{int32_t orderkey, double sum_qty, int32_t order_row_idx, int32_t cust_row_idx, double o_totalprice, int32_t o_orderdate}>

parallelism.phase1_partitioning: partition lineitem rows into equal-sized ranges across threads; each thread scans sequentially and must handle orderkey boundaries (align morsel starts to orderkey group boundaries using l_orderkey sorted order)

indexes.purpose: O(1) lookup: qualifying orderkey → orders row index
O(1) lookup: o_custkey → customer row index

late_materialization.c_name: Only decode c_name (varlen string from offsets+data files) for final top-100 rows
